Part B.key_evidence: PS1 nearest source at 0.41" with sgscore1=0.999 and stellar PS1 magnitudes (g=18.96, r=18.83, i=18.60, z=18.59) indicates a point-like stellar counterpart. Reference shows a clear point source at center; science shows a similar point source with a positive residual in the difference. sharpnr=-0.007 and chinr=0.269 are consistent with a clean PSF star. ndethist=3 over deltajd=480 days suggests sporadic low-amplitude detections at this position.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Low-amplitude brightening of a known PS1 stellar source, consistent with a Galactic variable star. The positive subtraction at the exact location of a high-confidence PS1 star, clean DAOphot shape parameters, and repeated detections over >1 year at the same position support this interpretation.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative is a cataclysmic-variable-like or faint AGN-like source; however, sgscore1~1.0 and stellar PS1 colors argue against an extended/AGN host. A supernova is disfavored because the counterpart is clearly a point-source star, not a galaxy, and the magpsf change is modest relative to the bright host.
